game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: R-TYPE is a classic horizontal side-scrolling shoot 'em up where players control a spaceship, navigating through 8 distinct levels filled with waves of enemies and culminating in a unique boss battle. Players move their ship by dragging a finger across the screen in "Enhanced Touch mode" or by using a virtual D-pad on the left side of the screen, while tapping on the right side fires the main weapon and holding it charges a powerful beam shot. The core mechanic involves managing the detachable "Force" pod, which can be attached to the ship for frontal or rear defense and offense, or launched independently to clear paths and absorb enemy fire. Collecting power-ups dropped by specific enemies upgrades the ship's main weapon, adds secondary weapons, and enhances the Force pod's capabilities, with difficulty scaling across two settings and within levels by increasing enemy projectile density and movement speed. The player has a limited number of lives, typically 3, and the game ends upon losing all lives, with the objective being to complete all 8 challenging levels.
game_info.description: Navigate your R-TYPE spaceship through intense waves of alien enemies across multiple levels. Manage your detachable Force pod - attach it for protection or launch it as an independent weapon. Collect power-ups to upgrade your weapons and abilities. Survive increasingly difficult enemy patterns and defeat the boss at the end of each level. Complete all levels with your limited lives to achieve victory.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Move spaceship in all directions
Space: Fire main weapon (hold to charge powerful beam shot)
Z: Detach/Attach Force pod
Shift: Launch Force pod forward
Enter: Start game
ESC: Pause/Unpause game
R: Restart from game over screen

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic ship movement, shooting mechanics, and Force pod attachment/detachment. Verifying that player controls are responsive and the Force pod can be manipulated correctly. Testing collision detection with enemies and power-up collection.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Move the ship in all directions using arrow keys. Fire continuously with space bar. Detach and reattach the Force pod multiple times using Z key. Launch the Force pod forward with Shift. Collect power-ups by moving into them. Engage with enemy waves to test collision.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Ship should move smoothly in all directions. Bullets should fire consistently and charge beam should activate when holding space. Force pod should detach/attach correctly and launch forward. Power-ups should be collectible and upgrade weapons. Collisions should register properly. Test is successful when all basic mechanics work without errors.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing win condition by automatically progressing through levels and defeating bosses. Verifying that level transitions work correctly and the game can reach a win state. Testing that the game handles multiple levels and boss encounters properly.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Automatically navigate the ship to avoid obstacles while continuously firing. Quickly eliminate all enemies in each wave. Focus fire on boss weak points. Progress through multiple levels rapidly. Collect all power-ups to maximize firepower.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The ship should successfully complete multiple levels. Boss battles should trigger at the end of each level. Defeating bosses should transition to the next level. After completing all levels, the game should enter GAME_OVER_WIN state. Test is successful when the win condition is reached and displayed properly.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing lose condition by allowing the ship to take damage and lose all lives. Verifying that health system works correctly, lives decrement properly, and game over state triggers when all lives are lost.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Allow the ship to collide with enemies and enemy projectiles repeatedly. Do not dodge incoming attacks. Let the ship take continuous damage until health reaches zero. Respawn and repeat until all lives are depleted.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Ship health should decrease with each hit. When health reaches zero, a life should be lost and ship should respawn. After losing all three lives, the game should enter GAME_OVER_LOSE state. Test is successful when game over screen appears with appropriate message after all lives are lost.

TEST_4.test_description: Testing weapon upgrade system and power-up variety. Verifying that different power-ups provide different upgrades and that the weapon system scales properly with collected power-ups.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Systematically collect all types of power-ups that appear. Test each weapon upgrade level by firing after collection. Use charged beam at different upgrade levels. Verify visual and functional differences between upgrade tiers.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Each power-up type should provide distinct upgrades (weapon level, missiles, speed boost). Weapon fire patterns should change visibly with upgrades. Charged beam should become more powerful with upgrades. Test is successful when all power-up types work correctly and weapon progression is smooth.

TEST_5.test_description: Testing Force pod mechanics including independent movement, damage dealing, and shield functionality. Verifying that the Force pod can absorb enemy fire and damage enemies effectively.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Launch Force pod into enemy formations. Position Force pod between ship and enemy projectiles. Test Force pod's ability to destroy enemies independently. Reattach Force pod at various angles. Use Force pod as both offensive weapon and defensive shield.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Force pod should destroy enemies on contact. It should absorb enemy projectiles when positioned defensively. Force pod should move independently when launched. Reattachment should be smooth and provide immediate protection. Test is successful when Force pod fulfills both offensive and defensive roles effectively.

TEST_6.test_description: Testing boss battle mechanics including boss patterns, weak points, and phase transitions. Verifying that boss encounters are challenging but fair, and that defeating bosses properly transitions to the next level.
TEST_6.strategy_description: Engage the boss and observe attack patterns. Focus fire on boss weak points. Dodge boss projectiles while maintaining offensive pressure. Test different weapon types against the boss. Defeat the boss to trigger level transition.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: Boss should have distinct attack patterns that are challenging but dodgeable. Boss health should decrease when hit, particularly at weak points. Boss should be defeated after taking sufficient damage. Level should transition smoothly after boss defeat. Test is successful when boss mechanics feel balanced and rewarding.

TEST_7.test_description: Testing difficulty progression across levels including enemy density, projectile patterns, and movement speed increases. Verifying that the game becomes appropriately more challenging as levels progress.
TEST_7.strategy_description: Progress through multiple levels and compare difficulty. Note increases in enemy count, projectile speed, and attack frequency. Test survival time in early vs late levels. Observe enemy movement patterns and formation complexity.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: Later levels should have more enemies on screen simultaneously. Enemy projectiles should move faster and appear more frequently. Enemy formations should be more complex. The challenge should scale gradually without sudden difficulty spikes. Test is successful when difficulty progression feels natural and engaging.
